name: Creamy, Fudgy, and Nutty Brownies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup chopped walnuts or pecans, toasted
3 ounces semi-sweet or bitter-sweet chocolate, chopped (2)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, melt unsweetened chocolate and margarine or butter over low heat, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat; cool.
2. Grease and lightly flour an 8x8x2-inch baking pan; set aside.
3. In a medium mixing bowl, stir together flour, chopped walnuts or pecans, and baking powder; set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the cooled melted chocolate mixture and sugar. Add eggs and vanilla. Using a wooden spoon, lightly beat until combined (do not overbeat).
5. Stir in the flour mixture. Pour batter into prepared pan; spread to edges.
6. Bake at 350°F for 40 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, melt 3 ounces semi-sweet or bitter-sweet chocolate in a small saucepan over low heat. Cool slightly.
8. In a medium mixing bowl, beat cream cheese on medium speed until softened. Add melted chocolate, egg, sugar, milk, and vanilla. Beat until combined.
9. Carefully spread topping evenly over hot brownies.
10. Bake at 350°F for an additional 10 minutes or until the topping is set. Cool in pan on a wire rack; cover and chill at least 2 hours before serving.
11. Cut into triangles or bars. Melt 2 squares chocolate and shortening (optional) in a small saucepan, drizzle over brownies and plate. Garnish with raspberries (optional). Cover and refrigerate to store.
